use_case_name: Set / Update External Charging Limit Without Ongoing Transaction

structured.name: Set / Update External Charging Limit Without Ongoing Transaction
structured.objective:
To inform the CSMS of a charging schedule or charging limit imposed by an external system on
the Charging Station for new transactions or on the grid connection.
structured.description:
To inform the CSMS of a charging schedule or charging limit imposed by an external system on
the Charging Station for new transactions or on the grid connection. The External Control System
can be a DSO, but also a smart meter or a home energy management system. The interface
between External Control System and Charging Station is not specified. It can be any protocol that
is supported by Charging Station for this purpose, even OCPP.
scenario: 1. External Control System sends a charging limit to Charging Station (not during a transaction).
2. Optional: Charging Station calculates new charging schedule.
3. Charging Station adjusts the charging speed.

4. If the charging limit changed by more than: LimitChangeSignificance, the Charging
Station sends a NotifyChargingLimitRequest message to CSMS with optionally the set charging
limit/schedule.
5. The CSMS responds with a NotifyChargingLimitResponse to the Charging Station.
structured.prerequisites:
Charging Station is not in error state.
The external system can set/clear a charging limit/schedule on the Charging Station via a direct
connection to the Charging Station.
structured.postconditions:
New transactions will be limited by the received charging limit from the external system.
The CSMS is informed of the new limit/schedule imposed by the external system.
structured.remarks:
The external system could, for example, use IEC 61850 [IEC61850-7-420] or OpenADR [OPENADR]
to communicate the grid limit to the Charging Station, but this could be any protocol. Furthermore,
an example of an external system is given, in this case a DSO that might set an external charging
limit in case of grid problems, but this could be any other external system or reason to set a
charging limit.

requirements.precondition:
When an external charging
limit/schedule is received
while no transactions are
ongoing
requirements.definition:
The total load of all EVSEs SHALL NOT exceed this given
limit.

requirements.precondition:
K12.FR.01
AND
Charging limit changed by
more than:
LimitChangeSignifica
nce
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL inform the CSMS of the new
charging limit/schedule imposed by the external system
by sending a NotifyChargingLimitRequest.

requirements.precondition:
K12.FR.02
AND
EnableNotifyCharging
LimitWithSchedules is
true
requirements.definition:
The NotifyChargingLimitRequest SHALL contain the
charging limit/schedule as set by the external system.

The Charging Station SHALL NOT set the
chargingLimitSource to CSO in the
NotifyChargingLimitRequest.

requirements.precondition:
When an external charging
limit/schedule is received
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL use purpose
ChargingStationExternalConstraints when reporting
about this limit (i.e. in a ReportChargingProfilesRequest).

chunks.content:
# K12 - Set / Update External Charging Limit Without Ongoing Transaction

**Objective:** To inform the CSMS of a charging schedule or charging limit imposed by an external system on
the Charging Station for new transactions or on the grid connection.

**Description:** To inform the CSMS of a charging schedule or charging limit imposed by an external system on
the Charging Station for new transactions or on the grid connection. The External Control System
can be a DSO, but also a smart meter or a home energy management system. The interface
between External Control System and Charging Station is not specified. It can be any protocol that
is supported by Charging Station for this purpose, even OCPP.

**Actors:** External Control System, Charging Station, CSMS

**Scenario:**
1. External Control System sends a charging limit to Charging Station (not during a transaction).
2. Optional: Charging Station calculates new charging schedule.
3. Charging Station adjusts the charging speed.
4. If the charging limit changed by more than: LimitChangeSignificance, the Charging
Station sends a NotifyChargingLimitRequest message to CSMS with optionally the set charging
limit/schedule.
5. The CSMS responds with a NotifyChargingLimitResponse to the Charging Station.

**Prerequisites:** Charging Station is not in error state.
The external system can set/clear a charging limit/schedule on the Charging Station via a direct
connection to the Charging Station.

**Postconditions:** New transactions will be limited by the received charging limit from the external system.
The CSMS is informed of the new limit/schedule imposed by the external system.

**Remarks:** The external system could, for example, use IEC 61850 [IEC61850-7-420] or OpenADR [OPENADR]
to communicate the grid limit to the Charging Station, but this could be any protocol. Furthermore,
an example of an external system is given, in this case a DSO that might set an external charging
limit in case of grid problems, but this could be any other external system or reason to set a
charging limit.

metadata.heading: K12 - Set / Update External Charging Limit Without Ongoing Transaction
metadata.use_case_name: Set / Update External Charging Limit Without Ongoing Transaction

chunks.content:
# K12 - Requirements

**K12.FR.01**
Precondition: When an external charging
limit/schedule is received
while no transactions are
ongoing
The total load of all EVSEs SHALL NOT exceed this given
limit.

**K12.FR.02**
Precondition: K12.FR.01
AND
Charging limit changed by
more than:
LimitChangeSignifica
nce
The Charging Station SHALL inform the CSMS of the new
charging limit/schedule imposed by the external system
by sending a NotifyChargingLimitRequest.

**K12.FR.03**
Precondition: K12.FR.02
AND
EnableNotifyCharging
LimitWithSchedules is
true
The NotifyChargingLimitRequest SHALL contain the
charging limit/schedule as set by the external system.

**K12.FR.04**
Precondition: K12.FR.02
The Charging Station SHALL NOT set the
chargingLimitSource to CSO in the
NotifyChargingLimitRequest.

**K12.FR.05**
Precondition: When an external charging
limit/schedule is received
The Charging Station SHALL use purpose
ChargingStationExternalConstraints when reporting
about this limit (i.e. in a ReportChargingProfilesRequest).

metadata.heading: K12 - Requirements
